explicit_text: I was born in Tarímbaro, Michoacán, Mexico in 1998, and moved to Minnesota with my grandmother when I was three to join my parents. I spent my early childhood in Minnesota, then returned to Mexico with my grandmother at thirteen for her medical treatment, staying there for about five years. I worked in my uncle's fertilizer business and cherished experiences like the Carnival festival. In 2016, I came back to Minnesota, now living in Mounds View, and finished my senior year of high school while adjusting to life back in the U.S. I feel grateful for living in two countries and the opportunities it has given me.
implicit_text: I was born in a small town in Michoacán, Mexico in 1998, and moved to the northern U.S. state with my grandmother when I was three to join my parents. I spent my early childhood there, then returned to Mexico with my grandmother at thirteen for her medical treatment, staying there for about five years. I worked in my uncle's fertilizer business and cherished experiences like the February festival. In 2016, I came back to the northern U.S. state, now living in a suburb north of the Twin Cities, and finished my senior year of high school while adjusting to life back in the U.S. I feel grateful for living in two countries and the opportunities it has given me.

explicit_text: Tarímbaro is the small town in Michoacán, Mexico where I was born in 1998. Although I moved to Minnesota when I was very young, I returned there as a teenager to live with my grandmother during her medical treatment. Life in Tarímbaro was very different from the U.S.; the houses were mostly brick and unpainted, and the community was close-knit. I enjoyed local traditions like the Carnival festival, which brought people from nearby towns together every February. Living there helped me reconnect with my roots and appreciate Mexican culture.
implicit_text: The small town in Michoacán, Mexico where I was born in 1998 holds a special place in my heart. Although I moved to the northern U.S. state when I was very young, I returned there as a teenager to live with my grandmother during her medical treatment. Life in that town was very different from the U.S.; the houses were mostly brick and unpainted, and the community was close-knit. I enjoyed local traditions like the February festival, which brought people from nearby towns together every year. Living there helped me reconnect with my roots and appreciate Mexican culture.

explicit_text: Michoacán is the Mexican state where I was born and where Tarímbaro is located. I spent part of my childhood there after moving back from Minnesota when I was thirteen. During those years, I adapted to school in Spanish and worked in my uncle's fertilizer business. Michoacán's busy cities and local customs like the Carnival festival made a lasting impression on me. Returning to Minnesota later, I carried with me a deep appreciation for my time in Michoacán.
implicit_text: The Mexican state where I was born and where my hometown is located shaped much of my early life. I spent part of my childhood there after moving back from the northern U.S. state when I was thirteen. During those years, I adapted to school in Spanish and worked in my uncle's fertilizer business. The state's busy cities and local customs like the February festival made a lasting impression on me. Returning to the northern U.S. state later, I carried with me a deep appreciation for my time there.

explicit_text: Minnesota has been my home since I was a toddler, after moving there with my grandmother to join my parents. I grew up in Minnesota, starting school there and learning English with the help of friends. After spending several years back in Mexico, I returned to Minnesota in 2016 to finish high school. Living in Minnesota has shaped much of my identity, and I plan to build my future here. The experience of moving between Minnesota and Mexico has given me a unique perspective on life.
implicit_text: This northern U.S. state has been my home since I was a toddler, after moving there with my grandmother to join my parents. I grew up there, starting school and learning English with the help of friends. After spending several years back in Mexico, I returned in 2016 to finish high school. Living there has shaped much of my identity, and I plan to build my future in this state. The experience of moving between this state and Mexico has given me a unique perspective on life.

explicit_text: I lived in St. Paul, Minnesota during my early years after moving from Mexico. I remember my first day of kindergarten there, feeling nervous because I didn't understand English. Thankfully, friends like Luis and Raina helped me learn the language quickly. I enjoyed going to school and spending time with my friends in St. Paul. It was a welcoming place that helped me grow during my childhood.
implicit_text: I lived in the capital city of the northern U.S. state during my early years after moving from Mexico. I remember my first day of kindergarten there, feeling nervous because I didn't understand English. Thankfully, friends like Luis and Raina helped me learn the language quickly. I enjoyed going to school and spending time with my friends in that city. It was a welcoming place that helped me grow during my childhood.

explicit_text: While living in Mexico, I noticed that many people got around using the Metrobús, taxis, or by walking, which was quite different from how we traveled in Minnesota. The Metrobús was a common mode of transportation in the busy city where I lived. It was part of the daily life and culture there, contrasting with the car-centric lifestyle I was used to in the U.S. Observing this helped me appreciate the differences between the two countries.
implicit_text: While living in Mexico, I noticed that many people got around using the rapid bus system, taxis, or by walking, which was quite different from how we traveled in the northern U.S. state. This rapid bus was a common mode of transportation in the busy capital city where I lived. It was part of the daily life and culture there, contrasting with the car-centric lifestyle I was used to in the U.S. Observing this helped me appreciate the differences between the two countries.

explicit_text: Carnival was my favorite holiday when I lived in Mexico, celebrated every February in the town near where I stayed. It was a big festival that attracted people from nearby towns and even TV channels that came to record the event. The day was crowded and lively, with fundraisers for a Carnival queen and drinks flowing all day. Participating in Carnival made me feel connected to the community and brought joy during my years in Mexico.

explicit_text: After returning to Minnesota in October 2016, I began living in Mounds View, which was different from my childhood home in St. Paul. Starting my senior year of high school there was challenging because I had no friends and had to adjust quickly. Despite the difficulties, I was grateful to be back and focused on finishing my education and planning my future. Mounds View became the place where I decided to settle permanently and pursue my career goals.

This suburb became the place where I decided to settle permanently and pursue my career goals.
